title: Immunotherapy — scope-broadening overclaim
notes: Synthetic; controlled ground truth. Overclaim exemplar: benefit shown only in a subgroup, claim generalises to all patients while the reference is silent on the rest. Uses bracketed citations. VERIFY before publishing.
manuscript: In patients with PD-L1-high tumours, nivolumab improved objective response rate versus chemotherapy [1]. Nivolumab improves response across all patients regardless of PD-L1 status [1]. Treatment-related deaths were rare [2].

goldClaims.originalText: In patients with PD-L1-high tumours, nivolumab improved objective response rate versus chemotherapy [1].
goldClaims.text: In patients with PD-L1-high tumours, nivolumab improved objective response rate versus chemotherapy

goldClaims.originalText: Nivolumab improves response across all patients regardless of PD-L1 status [1].
goldClaims.text: Nivolumab improves response across all patients regardless of PD-L1 status

1.name: CHECK-1 (Mori 2023)
1.text: Among patients with PD-L1-high tumours, the objective response rate was higher with nivolumab than chemotherapy (48% vs 27%). Outcomes in PD-L1-low patients were not reported in this analysis.

2.name: CHECK-1 safety
2.text: Treatment-related deaths occurred in fewer than 1% of patients in each arm.

goldVerdicts.claimText: In patients with PD-L1-high tumours, nivolumab improved objective response rate versus chemotherapy
goldVerdicts.rationale: Ref states ORR 48% vs 27% in the PD-L1-high group explicitly.

goldVerdicts.claimText: Nivolumab improves response across all patients regardless of PD-L1 status
goldVerdicts.rationale: Ref supports benefit only in PD-L1-high and is silent on other patients; claim broadens scope to all patients (rule 3).
Ref states treatment-related deaths <1% in each arm — supports 'rare'.
